title: Repair oversized OrionMkII workspace layout
description: The active OrionMkII/default workspace layout in zeus-prefs.db was persisted with an 883-row extent, crushing the UI on :6443. Add a guard for pathological workspace row counts and restore the active layout row from the known-good backup.
close_reason: Restored OrionMkII/default layout from backup and added parser guard for oversized saved rows.

title: Premium compact RX2 VFO panel
description: Refine the RX2 dual VFO controls into a denser, premium control surface with clearer focus, TX, RX2, and listening affordances.
close_reason: Implemented premium compact RX2 VFO panel refinement in commit adae5466

title: Compact RX2 VFO controls and stitched tuning
close_reason: Implemented compact RX2 VFO controls and stitched VFO A tuning in commit 2727ba80

title: Improve RX2 VFO split and TX VFO safety
description: Make dual-RX frequency control and panadapter split more intuitive, support RX2 on another band, add TX VFO selection, and gate first TX on a changed band behind tune/warning safety.
close_reason: Implemented stitched RX2 VFO workflow in commit 9695a929

title: Fix panel drag collision behavior
description: Dragging a panel downward should jump/swap over occupied panels instead of continuously pushing the rest of the layout down. Investigate the workspace grid drag/collision settings and adjust behavior so panel movement matches operator expectations.
close_reason: Fixed: workspace grid now uses no-compaction with collision prevention so dragged panels jump over occupied slots instead of pushing neighbors down. Verified with focused Vitest test, SPA build, and Vite browser smoke.

title: Align LAN desktop view with Photino desktop UI
description: The LAN desktop URL https://127.0.0.1:6443/ should render the same desktop UI as the Photino desktop app. Investigate and fix any host, bundle, route, or viewport gate divergence causing different views.
close_reason: Fixed: desktop-host loopback views now force the desktop shell and CSS guard; verified with focused Vitest coverage and SPA build.

title: Implement RX2 dual receive UI and routing
description: Add RX2 controls for VFO/frequency operation, split the panadapter when RX2 is enabled, and support audio monitor modes for RX1, RX2, or both. Reference Thetis RX2 behavior from C:\Users\Admin\Desktop\Thetis-master.
close_reason: Implemented RX2/VFO B state, DSP dual receive routing, split panadapter UI, and RX audio source controls.

title: Stabilize auto-notch validation and lock
description: Refine the ANF-driven auto-notch tracker so it dynamically samples candidate center/width stability, validates before emitting a notch, locks verified notches to stable center/width values, and rejects voice-like wandering energy to reduce false positives.
notes: Also gate NotchOverlay resize handles on the NOTCH armed state so existing notch bands do not intercept normal panadapter/waterfall clicks unless the operator explicitly enables notch editing.
close_reason: Auto-notch tracker now dynamically samples candidate stability, locks verified center/width values, rejects wandering voice-like candidates before validation, and NotchOverlay editing is gated on NOTCH armed state. Targeted frontend tests passed; full typecheck is still blocked by existing unused-symbol errors in signal-estimator.ts.

title: Implement real HDR waterfall extraction logic
description: User feedback: POP and normal waterfall still look essentially the same. Implement performant signal-processing logic, not just color/shader changes: adaptive dynamic-range mapping, multi-scale local contrast, coherence-aware weak-signal extraction, and a distinct normal HDR waterfall path.
notes: Implemented first dual-field topographic waterfall pass. Estimator now emits an optional 0..1 terrain/evidence height row in the same enhancement pass for both POP and normal RX. Waterfall renderer now maintains a synchronized second R32F history texture for terrain height, remaps/shifts it with the same frame-plan logic, and uploads zero terrain for TX/keyed rows. Shader samples terrain independently from dB/POP value, computes hillshade/contours/shadows/rims from screen-pixel height-field offsets, clamps POP summit saturation, and fixes valid-history sampling so unfilled slow-scroll areas seed dark instead of repeating the oldest row into vertical streaks. Focused tests pass (89/89), production build passes, desktop output serves assets/index-DILsRHCi.js, live diagnostics ready-trace 8/8 no hard blockers. Visual acceptance still pending on user's full live waterfall after rows accumulate; local POP toggle sample confirms no runtime errors but needs on-air feedback for whether terrain relief is visually strong enough.

title: Make POP waterfall depth visually falsifiable
description: User still sees no visible difference after the relief shader/default migration. Replace subtle per-pixel lighting with an unmistakable displaced outline/offset glow treatment that is obvious on live waterfall screenshots, and verify the live bundle/settings again.
notes: Scope widened after user feedback: normal waterfall should also get cutting-edge dynamic range/relief, while POP remains the stronger weak-signal extraction mode. Implement baseline HDR lift plus subtle edge relief; POP gets stronger alpha-visible displaced outline.
close_reason: Made the waterfall visual change falsifiable: normal mode now has HDR lift plus subtle relief, POP has alpha-visible offset shadow/outline glow, live app serves index-C5LXRaha.js with the new shader, POP prefs restored, Waterfall tests and frontend build passed.

title: Continue NR5 live weak-signal refinement with NR2 ideas
description: Continue sampling live NR5 diagnostics, study NR2/EMNR behavior for useful weak-signal/noise-floor ideas, and apply scoped NR5 refinements that improve weak-signal recovery without pumping or noise-only false-open behavior.
notes:
2026-06-17 G2 auto-phone cluster refinement on main: tightened tools/run-dsp-g2-rx-peak-hunt.ps1 so -AutoPhoneCluster now defaults to the 20m phone subband, 14.150-14.350 MHz, instead of mining the full 20m band where digital/CW-style reports at 14.074/14.076 could outrank the user-observed SSB phone activity trend. Strict validation and summary now propagate g2RxPeakHuntAutoPhoneClusterMinSpeechSamples, BandLowHz, and BandHighHz, and Markdown reports show the enabled/candidates/lookback/band line. Focused synthetic G2 validation fixture asserts the 14.150-14.350 MHz range in validator JSON, summary JSON, and Markdown. Live no-retune smoke tmp/20260617T145022898Z-g2-auto-phone-subband-cluster-smoke succeeded on auto-discovered http://127.0.0.1:6080: ok=true, bandLow=14150000, bandHigh=14350000, VFO restored 14260000, auto candidates=12, no 14.074/14.076, retained 14.240/14.270/14.277/14.300 plus the earlier active 20m phone candidates. Verification: PowerShell parser OK for run/validate/summarize scripts, plan-only auto cluster reports the phone-subband range, focused G2/validation dotnet slice passed 13/13 with OutDir bin/TestG2PeakHuntPhoneSubband, git diff --check clean. No DSP runtime/default/public API/TX/PureSignal behavior changed. Remaining gate before DSP default/enhancement promotion: capture a real mixed weak+strong G2 NR5/SPNR window, promote through live history, compare current-Zeus/Thetis-parity, then cross-radio/TX acceptance.
2026-06-17 G2 auto phone-cluster neighbor scouting on main: run-dsp-g2-rx-peak-hunt now reserves 25%/max-4 AutoPhoneCluster slots for inferred adjacent 3/5/7/10 kHz phone neighbors once recent 20m phone evidence exists, reports autoPhoneClusterMinSpeechSamples, exact candidate count, neighbor candidate count, and restores radio LO before VFO on pass/final retune restore. Strict validation/summary/test coverage now surfaces g2RxPeakHuntAutoPhoneClusterExactCandidateCount, NeighborCandidateCount, radio-LO restore safety, and hardware original/restored radio LO. Verification: PowerShell parser OK for run/validate/summarize, focused dotnet validation slice passed 13/13 with OutDir bin/TestG2PeakHuntNeighborCounts, git diff --check clean aside from existing LF/CRLF warnings. Live auto-discovered G2 hunt tmp/20260617T150416220Z-g2-auto-phone-neighbor-retune-hunt/g2-rx-peak-hunt-report.json succeeded on http://127.0.0.1:6080 with ok=true, VFO+radio LO restored, 12 auto candidates = 9 exact + 3 inferred neighbors (14.256, 14.274, 14.238 MHz), weak=93, strong=0, speechWeak=17, mixedReady=false. Targeted operator run for 14.240/14.270/14.277/14.300 captured partial per-window files but exited before aggregate report; partial windows still showed weak input and 0 strong-input samples, so no mixed weak+strong promotion yet. No DSP runtime/default/public API/TX/PureSignal behavior changed.

comments.author: Ramon Martinez
comments.text:
WDSP modernization slice: added tools/summarize-dsp-live-diagnostics-history.ps1 and documented it in docs/designs/wdsp-lineage-parity-analysis.md.

Purpose: review-only live tuning rollup after several NR5/NR2 attempts. It scans live-diagnostics-watch.json summaries, ranks latest/best weak-signal/lowest-pumping/best-balanced NR5 traces, emits JSON + Markdown, rolls safety signals by class, and reports latest-vs-previous NR5 deltas. No operator defaults, public APIs, native DSP behavior, or acceptance gates were changed.

Verification evidence:
- PowerShell parser check passed for summarize-dsp-live-diagnostics-history.ps1.
- Real live history rollup over live19/live20/live21/live28/live29/live30: traceCount=6, nr5TraceCount=4, readyTraceCount=6. Latest=20260616T040522495Z-nr5-live29-7175-balanced-makeup-direct-rescue with status=weak-and-pumping-watch. bestBalanced/bestWeak/lowestPumping all point to 20260616T032636099Z-nr5-live20-7175-voice-ab-after-nr2. Aggregate safety counts: pumping=18, weak-signal=4. Latest-vs-previous NR5 delta: weakRecoveryPct=-7.102, weakDropoutSampleCount=-3, nr5OutputMovementDb=-4, nr5MakeupMovementDb=-1.7, nr5RecoveryDriveMovement=-0.008, safetyRiskScore=-120.
- Deterministic synthetic fixture passed: traceCount=2, nr5TraceCount=2, latestStatus=weak-and-pumping-watch, bestBalanced=trace-a, aggregatePumping=6, aggregateWeak=2, Markdown contained Trace Summary. The temporary codex-history fixture was removed safely under tmp.
- git diff --check passed for the touched tool/doc paths; Git emitted only the existing LF-to-CRLF warning for docs/designs/wdsp-lineage-parity-analysis.md.

Generated smoke reports tmp/live-history-*.json/md were removed. Existing untracked live trace directories and broader dirty NR5/SPNR work were preserved.

comments.author: Ramon Martinez
comments.text:
WDSP modernization slice: promoted live diagnostics history into the capture/bundle workflow.

Changes:
- Added live-diagnostics-history as an optional capture-manifest artifact from DspBenchmarkCaptureManifestService with kind diagnostics-history-json and source tools/summarize-dsp-live-diagnostics-history.ps1.
- new-dsp-artifact-manifest.ps1 now emits artifacts/live-diagnostics-history.json only when -IncludeOptionalArtifacts is used, and capture-dsp-modernization-bundle.ps1 README next steps include the -BundleDir history command.
- validate-dsp-modernization-bundle.ps1 now validates live-diagnostics-history reports: tool identity, traceCount, nr5TraceCount, readyTraceCount, latest/best trace presence, bundleRelativePaths, absolute path leakage, aggregate safety class counts, and top-level liveDiagnosticsHistory* evidence fields.
- summarize-dsp-live-diagnostics-history.ps1 now defaults scan root to the bundle when -BundleDir is supplied and emits scanRoot='.' for the bundle root so generated reports stay portable.
- WDSP roadmap docs now show the history artifact command and validator fields. Service/API tests now assert the optional artifact is advertised while modernization snapshots still include only required artifacts.

Verification:
- PowerShell parser checks passed for summarize-dsp-live-diagnostics-history.ps1, new-dsp-artifact-manifest.ps1, capture-dsp-modernization-bundle.ps1, and validate-dsp-modernization-bundle.ps1.
- Focused Zeus.Server.Tests with isolated OutDir passed 4/4: CaptureManifest_ReadyNr5ListsG2EvidenceArtifacts, GetBenchmarkCaptureManifest_ReturnsCurrentEvidenceChecklist, ModernizationSnapshot_BundlesBlockedEvidenceAndMissingInputs, ModernizationSnapshot_ReadyNr5IsSingleCaptureBundle. The first normal run was blocked by live OpenhpsdrZeus PID 100116 holding Zeus.Server.Hosting.dll; isolated OutDir avoided disturbing the running app.
- Synthetic scaffold check passed: default manifest emitted 0 history artifacts; -IncludeOptionalArtifacts emitted exactly one non-required live-diagnostics-history artifact with kind diagnostics-history-json and path artifacts/live-diagnostics-history.json.
- Synthetic bundle validation passed for a portable optional history report: ok=true, errors=0, liveDiagnosticsHistoryReady=true, traceCount=2, nr5TraceCount=2, bestBalanced=trace-a, pumping=6, weak-signal=2, bundleRelativePaths=true, absolutePathCount=0.
- Synthetic required malformed history report failed as expected with live-history-paths-not-bundle-relative and live-history-absolute-paths, exit=1, absolutePathCount=3.
- git diff --check passed for touched paths with only existing LF-to-CRLF warnings.

A read-only explorer sidecar reviewed the integration risks; its recommendations were incorporated for optional/required severity, bundle-relative path checks, scaffold behavior, and snapshot required-artifact expectations. No DSP runtime defaults, native DSP behavior, public APIs, or operator behavior changed.

comments.author: Ramon Martinez
comments.text:
WDSP modernization slice: added structured latest-NR5 promotion decisions to live diagnostics history.

Changes:
- summarize-dsp-live-diagnostics-history.ps1 now emits latestNr5Decision plus promotionDecision compatibility fields. The decision is scoped to candidate-comparison-only and includes status, promotable/candidatePromotionReady flags, recommended trace/role, reference trace, blocker count/classes/counts/signals, latest/best risk scores, risk delta vs best-balanced, and next action. It also adds a Markdown Promotion Decision section and compact trace fields for audioPeakMaxDbfs, audioFreshPct, rxMetersFreshPct, and safety-class counts.
- validate-dsp-modernization-bundle.ps1 now reads latestNr5Decision, surfaces liveDiagnosticsHistoryPromotion* fields, and warns when an optional history report says the latest trace is not promotable. If a history artifact is marked required and the latest trace is not promotable, validation fails with live-history-promotion-not-ready.
- wdsp-lineage-parity-analysis.md now documents latestNr5Decision as candidate-comparison-only evidence, not authorization for default DSP behavior changes.

Verification:
- PowerShell parser checks passed for summarize-dsp-live-diagnostics-history.ps1 and validate-dsp-modernization-bundle.ps1.
- Real live-history rollup over live29/live35/live36/live37: traceCount=4, nr5TraceCount=4, latest=live37, latestStatus=blocked, latestNr5Decision.status=blocked-hard-gate, promotable=false, blockerClasses=hard-gate/pumping/weak-signal, recommendedTrace=live35 lower-weak-gate as best-balanced, latestRisk=2280, bestBalancedRisk=160, riskDelta=2120. This preserves live35 as the current tuning reference instead of promoting live37.
- Direct synthetic checks passed: blocked latest trace produced status=blocked-weak-and-pumping, promotable=false, recommended trace-a, blockerClasses=pumping/weak-signal; clean single trace produced status=ready-for-candidate-comparison, promotable=true; Markdown contained Promotion Decision.
- Synthetic bundle validation passed for optional blocked history: ok=true, errors=0, liveDiagnosticsHistoryReady=true, liveDiagnosticsHistoryCandidatePromotionReady=false, status=blocked-weak-and-pumping, recommended=trace-a, blockerClasses=pumping/weak-signal, warning includes live-history-promotion-not-ready. Marking the same artifact required failed with only live-history-promotion-not-ready.
- git diff --check passed for touched paths with only existing LF-to-CRLF warnings.

A read-only explorer sidecar reviewed the decision shape and pitfalls. No native DSP behavior, public API, operator defaults, or default DSP behavior changed.

comments.author: Ramon Martinez
comments.text:
WDSP modernization slice: added per-trace candidate readiness and stricter live-history consistency validation.

Changes:
- summarize-dsp-live-diagnostics-history.ps1 now marks every trace with candidateComparisonReady/promotable, promotionBlockerCount, promotionBlockerClasses, and promotionBlockerClassCounts. It also emits readyNr5TraceCount and candidateReadyNr5TraceCount so review can distinguish benchmark-ready NR5 captures from traces safe enough to promote into the next candidate comparison.
- validate-dsp-modernization-bundle.ps1 now validates live-history rollups against trace records: traceCount, nr5TraceCount, readyTraceCount, readyNr5TraceCount, and candidateReadyNr5TraceCount. It also validates latestNr5Decision/promotionDecision alias consistency, defaultBehaviorChangeReady=false, promotable vs candidatePromotionReady, latestTraceId matching, blocker count/classes consistency, and per-trace readiness invariants.

comments.author: Ramon Martinez
comments.text:
WDSP modernization slice: quantified live-history readiness gaps for NR5/SPNR tuning review.

Changes:
- summarize-dsp-live-diagnostics-history.ps1 now emits schemaVersion=2 safety signals with thresholdDirection, unit/readinessGapUnit, readinessGap, readinessMargin, and readinessGapScore. Per-trace readinessGapSummary/safetyClassReadiness and top-level aggregateReadinessGaps/aggregateSafetyClassReadiness group misses by safety class and unit so dB, percent, samples, unitless, and boolean gaps are not mixed. Promotion blockers now copy the same readiness fields from the latest trace signals.
- validate-dsp-modernization-bundle.ps1 now recomputes readiness fields from safetySignals, validates per-trace and aggregate readiness rollups, validates aggregateSafetyClassCounts from trace signals, checks blocker copies against the full latest trace record, and surfaces liveDiagnosticsHistoryAggregateReadinessGaps plus readiness gap count/max fields. Schema v1 histories remain legacy-compatible; schema v2 histories require the gap fields.
- wdsp-lineage-parity-analysis.md documents the schema v2 readiness-gap evidence and validator summary fields.

Verification:
- PowerShell parser checks passed for summarize-dsp-live-diagnostics-history.ps1 and validate-dsp-modernization-bundle.ps1.
- Real live40-live48 history smoke produced schemaVersion=2, traceCount=9, latest=live48, status=blocked-pumping, blockerCount=1, aggregateReadinessGapRows=4, readinessGapSignalCount=24, readinessGapMax=7.9. Validator smoke had no readiness/aggregate/safety-count mismatches; only expected optional warnings live-history-promotion-not-ready and live-history-paths-not-bundle-relative.
- Synthetic smoke covered maximum, minimum, zero-maximum, and boolean failures: gaps 1.25, 2.5, 3, and 1 respectively. Clean validation had no readiness mismatches; tampering a signal readinessGap to -1 produced live-history-readiness-field-mismatch as expected.
- git diff --check passed for tracked touched paths with only existing LF-to-CRLF warnings.

No native DSP behavior, public API, operator defaults, or default DSP behavior changed. Temporary dsp-history-gap smoke directories were removed; existing untracked live trace/comparison artifacts were preserved.

comments.author: Ramon Martinez
comments.text:
WDSP modernization slice: added latest-vs-previous NR5 readiness-gap trend analysis to live diagnostics history.

Changes:
- summarize-dsp-live-diagnostics-history.ps1 now emits schemaVersion=3 and canonical latestVsPreviousNr5ReadinessGapTrend, plus a compatibility latestVsPreviousReadinessTrend alias. The trend compares latest vs previous NR5 safety signals by safety class, signal name, threshold direction, and readinessGapUnit, with detail statuses new-gap, resolved-gap, gap-narrowed, gap-widened, and unchanged. It also emits unit-safe readinessGapTrendBuckets with delta totals/min/max and renders a Markdown Latest Readiness Trend section.
- validate-dsp-modernization-bundle.ps1 now recomputes the trend from full traces records, requires it for schemaVersion>=3, validates canonical trend details/buckets/counts, checks the compatibility alias, and surfaces liveDiagnosticsHistoryReadinessTrend* fields.
- wdsp-lineage-parity-analysis.md documents schema v3 trend evidence and validation summary fields.

Bundle validator recomputed the same top-level trend and produced only expected optional warnings live-history-promotion-not-ready and live-history-paths-not-bundle-relative.

comments.author: Ramon Martinez
comments.text:
WDSP modernization slice: fixed live-history schema v7 trace ordering/provenance.

Changes:
- summarize-dsp-live-diagnostics-history.ps1 now derives traceSequenceUtc from the timestamped trace directory before completedUtc/file mtime fallback, emits canonical UTC ISO strings, uses sortKeySource=trace-directory-timestamp, carries traceSequenceUtc/sortKeySource into compact traces, and applies deterministic traceId/path tie-breakers for latest/previous NR5 selection.
- validate-dsp-modernization-bundle.ps1 now supports schema v7 sequence validation: full and compact traces require valid traceSequenceUtc/sortKeySource, traces must be monotonic by sequence, compact provenance must match full records, latestTrace must be the highest-sequence NR5 trace, and previousNr5Trace must be the second-highest NR5 trace. Validation reports surface liveDiagnosticsHistoryLatestTraceSequenceUtc, liveDiagnosticsHistoryLatestTraceSortKeySource, liveDiagnosticsHistoryTraceOrderingStatus, and liveDiagnosticsHistoryTraceOrderingViolationCount.
- wdsp-lineage-parity-analysis.md documents schema v7, the directory/completedUtc/file-mtime precedence, and the NR5 latest/previous validation contract.

comments.author: Ramon Martinez
comments.text:
WDSP modernization slice: hardened live-history trace identity for bundle and matrix layouts.

Changes:
- summarize-dsp-live-diagnostics-history.ps1 now walks upward from each watch summary to find the nearest timestamped trace/capture ancestor instead of assuming the immediate parent is the trace ID.
- Matrix summaries under artifacts/live-diagnostics-traces/<scenario>/<comparison>/live-diagnostics-watch.json now get stable IDs like <timestamp>-<scenario>-<comparison>, preserving unique traces inside a shared capture bundle while retaining trace-directory-timestamp ordering.
- Non-timestamp layouts now fall back to a sanitized relative path identity and completedUtc sequencing instead of collapsing to generic parents such as artifacts or current-zeus.
- wdsp-lineage-parity-analysis.md documents the timestamped-ancestor behavior and matrix path suffix convention for schema v7.

Verification:
- PowerShell parser checks passed for summarize-dsp-live-diagnostics-history.ps1 and validate-dsp-modernization-bundle.ps1.
- Synthetic layout smoke using a real NR5 watch report produced three traces: 20260616T070000000Z-bundle with trace-directory-timestamp, 20260616T070000000Z-bundle-ssb-like-speech-dynamic-nr5-spnr with trace-directory-timestamp, and no-stamp fallback tmp-dsp-history-layout-smoke-no-stamp-bundle-weak-cw-carrier-current-zeus with completedUtc.
- BundleDir smoke over the timestamped bundle produced bundleRelativePaths=true and two trace IDs: 20260616T070000000Z-bundle and 20260616T070000000Z-bundle-ssb-like-speech-dynamic-nr5-spnr.
- Disposable bundle validation passed with ok=true, errors=0, liveDiagnosticsHistoryTraceOrderingStatus=sequence-ready, traceOrderingViolationCount=0, latestTraceId=20260616T070000000Z-bundle-ssb-like-speech-dynamic-nr5-spnr, and no live-history sequence/sort-key issue codes. Expected optional warnings remained external-engine-candidates-missing, live-history-promotion-not-ready, and artifact-file-entry-missing.
- git diff --check passed for touched paths with only the existing LF/CRLF warning.

No native DSP behavior, operator defaults, public APIs, TX path, or default DSP behavior changed. Temporary dsp-history-layout smoke/bundle artifacts were removed; existing untracked live trace and comparison artifacts were preserved.

comments.author: Ramon Martinez
comments.text:
WDSP modernization slice: added schema v8 live experiment coverage to diagnostics history.

comments.author: Ramon Martinez
comments.text:
NR5 behavior refinement pass complete for 14.230 MHz USB live diagnostics.

Implemented:
- native/wdsp/spnr.c: added NR5 speech-valley continuity support with a stricter core path plus a weak-tail path gated by confidence, AGC gate, weak-signal memory, recovery drive, mask smoothing, and low-evidence suppression. Goal is to preserve syllable tails/weak fragments without globally lifting the floor.
- Zeus.Server.Hosting/DspPipelineService.cs: made the RX audio leveler NR5-aware in two ways: it no longer restores an NR5-muted noise floor, and uncertain NR5 weak-tail frames now get an evidence-weighted boost ceiling so they do not request full global leveler gain.
- tests/Zeus.Server.Tests/DspPipelineAudioSanitizerTests.cs: added coverage for NR5 muted floor suppression and uncertain speech-tail boost capping.
- Deployed final win-x64 wdsp.dll SHA256 c0ae8ca31918a02b99844271636242ad21db2ade7d5b120c6dc951fe7b4dce5f.

Live evidence:
- live182 baseline after squelch-off: weakRecovered 42/70, weakDropout 19, weakBelowInput 9, lowEvidenceLifted 3/3, levelerMax 29.9 dB.
- live183 with NR5-aware leveler: weakRecovered 43/70, weakDropout 18, weakBelowInput 2, lowEvidenceLifted 0/3, levelerMax 22.2 dB.
- live184 with first speech-valley native change: weakRecovered 36/70, weakDropout 18, weakBelowInput 2, lowEvidenceLifted 0/2, alignment mismatch improved to 4.3%, levelerMax 21.2 dB.
- live186 with continuity-tail native tune during a lower-probability window: weakDropout improved to 4, weakNearTarget 35, but lowEvidenceLifted 4/17 and levelerMax 25.6 dB showed the leveler needed a tighter uncertainty cap.
- final live187 after managed evidence cap: 70/70 ready, squelch off, no output limiting, lowEvidenceLifted 0/21, weakDropout 4, levelerMax 20.5 dB, constrained leveler samples 5. This trace was a weaker/less speech-like window (probAvg 0.167, confAvg 0.312, gateAvg 0.501), so it is mainly a noise-floor guardrail rather than proof of full weak-voice recovery.

Verification:
- dotnet test tests/Zeus.Server.Tests/Zeus.Server.Tests.csproj --filter "FullyQualifiedName~DspPipelineAudioSanitizerTests|FullyQualifiedName~DspLiveDiagnosticsServiceTests" -p:UseSharedCompilation=false passed 40/40.
- dotnet test tests/Zeus.Dsp.Tests/Zeus.Dsp.Tests.csproj --filter "FullyQualifiedName~NoiseReductionTests|FullyQualifiedName~Nr5SyntheticFixtureTests|FullyQualifiedName~WdspNativeLoaderTests" -p:UseSharedCompilation=false passed 145, skipped 120.
- tools/audit-wdsp-runtime-artifacts.ps1 -FailOnMissingWinX64Nr5 -JsonOnly passed; win-x64 NR5 ready with final hash c0ae8ca3..., non-win-x64 RIDs still pending NR5 rebuild as expected.
- tools/audit-wdsp-native-symbols.ps1 -BinaryPath Zeus.Dsp/runtimes/win-x64/native/wdsp.dll -RequireBinaryExports -JsonOnly passed: readyForReview=true, binaryExportCount=539, no missing required exports, no signature mismatches.
- git diff --check on touched behavior/test files passed with LF/CRLF warnings only.

Issue remains in progress. Current best interpretation: NR5 now avoids re-amplifying low-evidence noise and preserves more weak-tail fragments, but matched-window speech evidence is still needed before claiming the weak-signal voice recovery target is solved.

comments.author: Ramon Martinez
comments.text:
Continuation NR5 20m live refinement with NR2/EMNR decision-memory idea folded into NR5.

Implemented:
- native/wdsp/spnr.c: added a mask-stable/decision-memory tail floor for weak voice valleys. It requires mask smoothing plus confidence and probability/weak-memory/recovery support, then lifts only toward a controlled sub-speech floor instead of globally raising noise.
- Zeus.Server.Hosting/DspPipelineService.cs: tightened the NR5-aware final RX leveler cap so low-confidence/low-probability frames with AGC gate <=0.54 and NR5 output <=-30 dBFS cannot be normalized to full speech loudness.
- tests/Zeus.Server.Tests/DspPipelineAudioSanitizerTests.cs: added live-derived coverage for low-evidence floor lift and borderline low-evidence tail capping.

Verification:
- Focused server tests passed: 42/42.
- Focused Zeus.Dsp NR/native tests passed: 145 passed, 120 skipped.
- Native build passed and deployed win-x64 wdsp.dll SHA256 5403be993613ff507bb16e5f4847e28e735ec5a430ff7fa4a832954ad23c1f00.
- Runtime artifact audit: readyForWinX64Package=true, pendingRidCount=4 expected for non-win-x64 NR5 rebuilds.
- Native symbol audit: readyForReview=true, binaryExportCount=539, no required source/binary misses.
- npm --prefix zeus-web run build passed with existing Vite chunk/dynamic-import warnings.
- git diff --check passed with LF/CRLF warnings only.

Live evidence:
- live188 fresh NR5 pre-pass on 14.230 USB: weakRecovered 25/70, weakDropout 2, weakBelowInput 3, lowEvidenceLifted 1/12, lowEvidenceSuppressed 8, levelerMax 17.9 dB.
- live189 NR2 reference: much lower leveler input and movement, but required ~36 dB final boost and has no NR5 weak-signal diagnostics.
- live190 first mask-tail/cap candidate: lowEvidenceLifted still 2/18 and weakDropout 3, so tightened the low-evidence leveler cap and broadened decision-memory tail support.
- final live191 on final DLL: 70/70 ready, squelch off, no output limiting/peak limiting, weakDropout 0, lowEvidenceLifted 0/46, lowEvidenceSuppressed 32, leveler constrained only 2/70. This trace had a long low-evidence/no-voice stretch, so weakRecovered was only 11/70 and weakBelowInput 43/70 by design; the voice burst near the end normalized cleanly while the noise-only floor stayed suppressed.

Issue remains in progress. Current interpretation: the latest pass fixes the two concrete failures from the previous live trace (low-evidence floor pumping and weak-tail dropout), but it deliberately refuses to make low-evidence noise full volume. More matched active-speech windows are still needed before calling NR5 done/perfect.

comments.text:

- Extended the adjacent-band profile path so frontend left/right adjacent bins and floors now reach native NR5 instead of being collapsed to only aggregate floor/spread/rejection.

One-sided/asymmetric adjacent evidence is deliberately down-weighted to avoid treating nearby QRM/splatter as clean noise.
- NR5 diagnostics schema bumped to v9 with native adjacent left/right bins, left/right floor, sideBalance, and asymmetryDb. Live diagnostics evidence and the hardware diagnostics panel now surface side balance/skew. The watcher summarizes native side balance/asymmetry and warns only when average side balance is genuinely low.

- live diagnostics: requested/effective Nr5, readyForNr5Tuning=true, wdspActive=true, filter=300-2600 Hz NR5-WEAK

Formal single-window Off vs NR5 comparator:
- Off baseline: tmp/20260616T211448482Z-off-live256-20m-14310000-no-nr-reference/20260616T211449774Z-off-live256-20m-14310000-no-nr-reference/live-diagnostics-watch.json
- NR5 candidate: tmp/20260616T211503545Z-nr5-live257-20m-14310000-post-off-reference/20260616T211504890Z-nr5-live257-20m-14310000-post-off-reference/live-diagnostics-watch.json
- report: tmp/20260616T211503545Z-nr5-live257-20m-14310000-post-off-reference/nr5-live257-vs-off-live256-14310000-comparison.json
- result: 5 formal regressions, 0 hard regressions, 0 gate failures, 3 improvements, 9 missing expected because Off lacks NR5-native metrics. The regressions were not accepted as NR5 algorithm regressions because the Off and NR5 windows had different live signal occupancy and Off lacks NR5 tuning fields.

Matched alternating A/B no-NR vs NR5 capture:
- summary: tmp/20260616T212049502Z-ab-live259-20m-14310000-off-vs-nr5/ab-live259-off-vs-nr5-summary.json
- method: 4 Off windows and 4 NR5 windows, each 8 samples at 250 ms, with NR5 restored afterward.
- Off: 32 samples, average final audio RMS -18.644 dBFS, active-audio 100%, AGC movement 0 dB, leveler constrained 4 samples, boost-slew 3, peak-limited 1.
- NR5: 32 samples, average final audio RMS -44.619 dBFS, active-audio 62.5%, floor-audio 37.5% with floor average -81.589 dBFS, voice-like average -25.694 dBFS, AGC movement 0 dB, leveler constrained 1 sample, boost-slew 1, peak-limited 0.
- Delta: NR5 average audio RMS was 25.975 dB lower than no NR, active-audio average was 5.082 dB lower, leveler applied-gain movement was 2.3 dB lower, and constrained samples improved by 3.
- NR5 weak preservation: 30 weak-input NR5 samples, 7 strict weak-dropout rows, all 7 final-audio audible, candidateWeakLoss=0, hotMakeup=0.

Interpretation:
- Versus no NR, NR5 is now clearly reducing the perceived noise floor rather than behaving like bypass.
- The high NR5 full-trace RMS movement is mostly floor suppression between bursts; AGC movement stayed 0 dB and leveler constraints were lower than no NR.
- The current remaining issue is not proven weak-signal deletion in this no-NR A/B run; it is active-audio loudness still landing about 5 dB below raw no-NR noise/signal windows, so future refinement should focus on speech/weak-burst level normalization without lifting low-evidence floor.

title: Refine NR5 spectral hold to reduce remaining fade
description: After anti-fade pass, long-term AGC movement is reduced but live NR5 output still dips when spectral gain collapses between weak coherent frames. Add a bounded recent-signal spectral hold that preserves faint signal continuity without lifting noise-only frames.
close_reason: Refined NR5 weak-signal recovery and anti-fade behavior: added signal-evidence-driven AGC recovery and bounded fast makeup, tightened narrow-tone false-open guard, deployed tested WDSP DLL, and captured live diagnostics at tmp/nr5-final-weak-recovery-live.json showing output span 21.3 dB over a 25.9 dB input span with no -60 dBFS collapse. Tests: NoiseReductionTests.Wdsp_Nr5 4 passed; NoiseReductionTests|WdspNativeLoaderTests 138 passed / 120 skipped.

title: Make Pop Relief unmistakable in live waterfall
description: User still sees no visual difference with POP active. Confirm live settings and replace subtle relief color-grade with an obvious GPU emboss/halo/depth treatment that is visibly different at default settings.
close_reason: Made Pop Relief unmistakable in the live waterfall: stronger GPU bevel/halo shader, legacy 48/42 relief migration to 92/64, persisted live prefs updated, focused frontend/backend tests and builds passed.

title: Fix SetTXACFCOMPprofile P/Invoke signature mismatch
description: The new WDSP native symbol audit reports SetTXACFCOMPprofile as managed 7 params versus native 5 params in native/wdsp/cfcomp.c. Fix the C# binding/call surface to match the vendored WDSP/Thetis ABI without changing DSP defaults, then rerun the audit and focused/full tests.
close_reason: Fixed SetTXACFCOMPprofile P/Invoke to match the vendored WDSP 5-parameter ABI, removed the stale Q-pointer call/comment, reran the native symbol audit to readyForReview=true, and verified Zeus.Dsp.Tests plus full solution build/test.

title: Make Pop Relief waterfall visibly stronger and diagnose crash
description: User reports no visible difference from relief-shaded waterfall and screenshot shows OpenhpsdrZeus.exe unhandled exception dialog. Increase visual impact of Pop Relief, verify bundle/runtime path, and guard shader/runtime failure paths.
close_reason: Made Pop Relief visibly stronger with local contrast, rim highlights, cast shadows, stronger defaults, and updated the served debug webroot; diagnosed screenshot crash as duplicate LAN HTTPS :6443 bind and guarded desktop/server startup; tests/build/live bundle checks pass.

title: Audition remains enabled on transmit
description: Audio Suite audition should automatically turn off when the operator keys transmit. This used to happen and has regressed, so TX should clear audition/TX Monitor instead of leaving local monitor active during transmit.
close_reason: Fixed: TX start now clears Audio Suite audition/TX Monitor, with server and frontend store coverage.

title: Catch wide coherent auto-notch blockers
description: Enhance the auto-notch detector so ANF-driven auto notching catches strong, coherent wide blockers visible in live panadapter/waterfall diagnostics, including edge blockers and paired mid-span lines, without turning ordinary broad occupied signals into auto notches.
close_reason: Auto notch detector now accepts strong coherent wide blockers and edge-visible blockers while preserving broad real-signal rejection; targeted tests and typecheck pass.

title: Stop NR5 audio fading in and out
description: NR5 audio still audibly fades in/out after weak/strong leveler changes. Stabilize post-NR leveler/gate/limiter so confirmed signals hold steady without pumping, while noise-only frames are not lifted.
close_reason: NR5 anti-fade pass: added held level-drive state, slowed gate/envelope/gain motion, decoupled limiter from long-term AGC gain, softened weak-island spectral gain drops, rebuilt WDSP, verified NR5 and broader DSP slices, and sampled live P2 diagnostics with squelch/ANF isolated.

title: Make NR5 weak and strong signals equal loudness
description: NR5 post-normalizer still leaves weak and powerful signals at noticeably different loudness. Refine AGC/leveler so confirmed signals normalize toward the same perceived volume without reintroducing pumping or lifting noise-only frames.
close_reason: NR5 leveler now uses a confirmed-signal constant RMS target, 48x makeup ceiling, weak-island spectral protection, slew-limited gain, and final RMS limiter; tested with NR5 DSP slice, broader DSP slice, and live P2 diagnostics.

title: Fix Windows installer payload VST bridge verification
description: CI publish is failing in tools/verify-windows-installer-payload.ps1 when it loads Zeus.Plugins.Host.dll directly from the installer payload and cannot resolve System.Runtime 10.0.0. The verification needs to validate the payload using the published runtime context or otherwise resolve dependencies from the installer layout.

title: fix(tci): iq_samplerate echoes requested rate instead of actual delivery rate
description: Issue #570: HandleIqSampleRate echoes the client-requested rate verbatim even though the IQ stream is always delivered at the hardware sample rate. Clients that size their DSP pipeline on the echoed value silently get the wrong rate. Path A fix: echo the hardware rate (radio.Snapshot().SampleRate) so clients detect that their request wasn't honored. Same honesty fix applied to audio_samplerate (always 48000). The stored _iqSampleRate is kept for future Path B decimation.
close_reason: HandleIqSampleRate and HandleAudioSampleRate now echo the actual delivery rate; all tests green

title: feat(cw): decode CW server-side from RxAudioAvailable, stream text over hub (headless + desktop)
description: Pivot from PR1's client-side WebAudio decoder (broken: doesn't work in desktop/Photino mode where audio is rendered natively, and can't run headless). Move decoding to the server: a CwDecoderService (BackgroundService, singleton+hosted) subscribes to DspPipelineService.RxAudioAvailable (demodulated post-AGC RX audio, ~30Hz ticks, headless-safe — fires pre-sink regardless of any audio output). Runs an envelope-based key detector (energy variation above an adaptively-tracked noise floor, with Schmitt hysteresis — works for CW-on-SSB where there is no true silence between elements) + Morse FSM, gated on RxMode.CWL/CWU. Emits decoded text + wpm/snr/confidence via a new CwDecodedTextFrame (MsgType 0x31) broadcast by StreamingHub, mirroring the existing CwEngineStatusFrame (0x30) pattern. Frontend: ws-client handler dispatches to the existing cw-decoder-store.appendChar; client decoder.ts/useCwDecoderAudio.ts deleted. RED-LIGHT: touches Zeus.Contracts wire format + adds a service → needs maintainer (Brian/EI6LF) sign-off before merge. Supersedes zeus-67t (client timing fix).
close_reason: Delivered by PR #549 (cw-decoder-548 → develop): CwDecoderService + CwAudioDecoder server-side envelope/Morse decode on RxAudioAvailable, CwDecodedTextFrame (MsgType 0x31) over StreamingHub, ws-client dispatch to cw-decoder-store, client decoder deleted. Merged to develop.

title: fix(cw): decoder timing must derive from sample count, not performance.now()
description: Live validation on feature/cw-decoder-investigation: the CW decoder only emits 1-2 element chars (E/T/I), reports a pinned ~59 WPM (ditMs clamped to the 10ms floor), and shows ~1dB SNR. Root cause: CwDecoder.process() timestamps every sample with performance.now(); processSamples() iterates an entire ~960-sample frame in a microsecond burst, so all samples in a frame share one timestamp and dit/dah durations measure ~0. Fix: thread a sample-based clock (sampleIndex/sampleRate*1000) and rework the per-sample debounce accordingly. Secondary: AdaptiveThreshold cold-start (noiseFloor=0) reports everything as keyed for the first window, emitting a spurious 'E' on every on/off toggle — add a primed guard. Deferred (#3): block-based Goertzel + slower noise-floor tracker if per-sample magnitude proves too noisy.
close_reason: Superseded by zeus-dmx: decoding moves server-side; the sample-clock timing insight ports to C#.

title: nightly macOS signing fails: missing secrets: inherit in nightly.yml
description: The Nightly workflow (.github/workflows/nightly.yml) calls release.yml as a reusable workflow (uses:) without 'secrets: inherit'. release.yml's macOS signing step (gated on is-nightly=='true') reads secrets.MACOS_CERTIFICATE / _PWD / KEYCHAIN_PASSWORD, but a reusable-workflow call only sees repo secrets when the caller inherits them — so they arrive empty and 'security import' fails with 'SecKeychainItemImport: Unable to decode the provided data' (empty base64 .p12). Confirmed the secrets exist at repo level. Fix: add 'secrets: inherit' to the nightly job. Lives on main (schedule honours default branch only). Run: actions/runs/26388557106 job 77673178545.
close_reason: Merged to develop via PR #528 (secrets: inherit in nightly.yml)

title: fix(protocol-1): include TxFreq in non-MOX phase rotation so HL2 extamp boards emit Kenwood CAT on dial change
description:
GitHub issue: https://github.com/Kb2uka/openhpsdr-zeus/issues/361

## Symptom

Square SDR 2 (HL2-class, fw 75.52, classifies as HermesLite2) outputs no band voltage on ACC-pin-1 BVO under Zeus, regardless of the Band Volts (C3 bit 3) checkbox state. Same hardware + same connector follows the dial fine under deskHPSDR with the checkbox in either state. Operator: Colin (@41south), driving an Xiegu XPA125B.

## Root cause

Square SDR 2's gateware enables the upstream HL2 `extamp` module, which emits Kenwood CAT (`FA000XXXXXXXX;`) on `io_uart_txd` only when the FPGA's internal TX-frequency register changes value. A daughter STM32G031 parses that CAT on USART2 and drives BVO PWM (TIM2 CH4) + BCD GPIO out to the rear-panel ACC connector.

Zeus's `PhaseRegisters(phase, mox=false, psArmed=false)` in `Zeus.Protocol1/Protocol1Client.cs:965-971` rotates only `Config / RxFreq / DriveFilter / Attenuator`. `CcRegister.TxFreq` (C0=0x02) is **never** written while receiving — only when MOX is asserted (lines 957-963). Result: the FPGA's TX-NCO register stays static during RX dial changes, no CAT edge fires, BVO never updates.

deskHPSDR's `src/old_protocol.c:2837-2846` writes `output_buffer[C0] = 0x02` (DUC frequency) on every pass of its round-robin scheduler regardless of MOX state, which is what keeps the FPGA `extamp` edge alive.

## Fix sketch

Add `CcRegister.TxFreq` to the non-MOX, non-PS-armed rotation phase table so the TX-NCO is refreshed on every dial change while receiving. Mirror to the `psArmed && !mox` table (`Protocol1Client.cs:933-951`) if not already covered.

## Risk / red-light points

The Protocol-1 round-robin is load-bearing for every supported board (Metis, Hermes, ANAN, Orion-MkII family, HL2). Before merging:

1. Verify the new rotation doesn't displace a phase a non-HL2 board depends on.
2. Cross-check against mi0bot Thetis and ramdor Thetis that they also write TX-NCO during RX.
3. Bench-test on HL2 at 28400 RX-only to confirm no regression (no spurious BVO change, no panadapter glitch, no PS-arm misbehavior).
4. PR description must flag "scheduler change — non-HL2 boards untested locally, please bench-verify before merge" for maintainer.

## Out of scope

- BCD on ACC pins 11-14 — Colin offered to test once BVO works. Fix should light up both at once (same `extamp` → STM32 chain), but bench-confirm before claiming.
- C3 bit 3 (`EnableHl2BandVolts`) wire-write — already correct per audit; no change needed.

## Test plan

- HL2 RX: panadapter on 28400, dial sweep across band; no spurious behavior.
- HL2 MOX (dummy load only, 28400): no rotation regression; PA/drive cadence unchanged.
- Code read: confirm non-HL2 boards (Metis / Hermes / Orion-MkII variant) still receive a `Config` phase often enough not to lose nrx / dither / random settings.
- Ship a pre-release build for Colin to test on Square SDR 2 + XPA125B.

title: P1: Implement onboard codec audio path (Hermes RX headphones + TX mic-in)
description:
Reported in GH #426 against Hermes on v0.8.2: 'Onboard codec not worked'. Thetis / linHPSDR / piHPSDR all work on the same board.

Root cause (confirmed in code):
- Zeus.Protocol1/ControlFrame.cs:516-532, 562 — EP2 outbound 8-byte slot is [L s16 BE][R s16 BE][I s16 BE][Q s16 BE], and the L/R audio bytes are intentionally left zero. Comment: 'HL2 has no audio codec in the MVP target, so audio bytes stay zero'.
- Zeus.Protocol1/PacketParser.cs:240 — incoming mic samples at off+6,off+7 are read but discarded ('Unused for RX MVP').

On HL2 this was acceptable because operators use host audio (browser getUserMedia + WebAudio). On Hermes / Mercury / ANAN-class the radio's onboard codec IS the primary audio path — operators expect to plug a mic into the front-panel jack and listen on the radio's headphone jack. That entire path is unimplemented.

This is the headline failure in #426 and the most likely reason linoobs's Hermes is unusable in Zeus while it works fine in Thetis.
design: Gate via BoardCapabilitiesTable. Hermes / Mercury / ANAN-class default to onboard codec; HL2 defaults to host/browser audio. TX path: route mic source samples into EP2 L/R bytes (currently always zero in ControlFrame.WriteUsbFrame). RX path: extract the 16-bit mic-monitor bytes in PacketParser.TryParsePacket and surface via an IRxCodecAudioSource seam (parallel to IRxAudioSink for DSP-out). Operator UI toggle to override the per-board default. Coordinate with audio-native plan ([[project_audio_native_plan]]).
acceptance_criteria: On a Hermes board with mic plugged into the front-panel jack, transmitted SSB audio matches Thetis behaviour. RX audio plays out the front-panel headphone jack at a usable level. Behaviour is board-gated via BoardCapabilitiesTable so HL2 default (browser mic) is preserved.
notes: Phase A landed in commit (fix(p1) on fix/426-onboard-codec branch) — RX audio reaches the radio's headphone jack on Hermes / ANAN-class / OrionMkII / G2E boards via EP2 L/R bytes. HL2 unchanged (HasOnboardCodec=false). Phase B (radio mic → WDSP TXA) tracked in new bd item — see /api/recent for that item's ID; needs operator UI for mic-source selection.

title: Audio start does not load selected profile
description: Starting audio with a saved profile should load that profile's configured input/output state. Currently audio startup appears to ignore or misapply the selected profile.
notes:
Reopened after bench report: first transmission still starts before the selected TX/audio profile is applied. Investigating startup/connect path instead of manual selector path.
Final runtime check after process handoff: live OpenhpsdrZeus PID 26256 is listening on 127.0.0.1:6061 and connected to 192.168.1.25:1024 in LSB at 384 kHz. VST route remains active and master bypass is false. A final 60-sample Audio Suite chain meter check showed OUT min -43.4 dB, max -36.4 dB, and 0 near-full-scale samples (> -6 dB).
close_reason:
Fixed and verified.

TX/audio profile activation now runs outside the TX profile panel and is awaited by the first MOX/TUNE/PTT/key preflight before TX is keyed, so the selected station profile and saved Audio Suite profile are applied before first transmission.

VST meter logic was tightened so off-air live preview degraded/pass-through blocks no longer overwrite the last real VST engine OUT meter, while on-air TX still records honest pass-through meters if the engine degrades.

Live verification after rebuild/relaunch on 127.0.0.1:6061:
- Connected to 192.168.1.25:1024 at 48 kHz.
- Audio Suite route: vst, engineActive: true, masterBypassed: false.
- TX fidelity policy: essb, Audio Suite profile: VST x1.
- State mic gain -4 dB and leveler max 11 dB match the essb station profile.
- LSB TX filter is signed correctly: -4000..-40 Hz.
- 90 Audio Suite chain meter samples while connected: OUT min -43.5 dB, max -34.0 dB, 0 near-full-scale samples (> -6 dB). The prior fast OUT jump to about -1 dB did not recur.

Checks:
- Focused frontend TX profile tests: 16 passed.
- Frontend production build: passed.
- Frontend lint: passed with existing warnings.
- OpenhpsdrZeus backend build: passed 0 warnings / 0 errors.

Full-suite caveat: current dirty worktree has unrelated verification blockers filed as zeus-4lbg (Waterfall Vitest expectation and DisplayIntelligenceSettingsDto test constructor compile error), so full Vitest/dotnet test suites are not fully green in this worktree.

title: Add synthetic NR5 weak-signal audio fixtures
description: Add bench-quality synthetic fixtures for the experimental NR5/SPNR mode: buried tone in AWGN, weak SSB-like modulation, fading carrier, impulsive noise with NB off/on, and noise-only stability. Use them to compare NR5 against NR off, NR2, and NR4 without depending on on-air reports.
acceptance_criteria: Synthetic fixtures are committed; assertions cover signal preservation, output level normalization, clipping avoidance, and noise-only stability; NR5 results are compared with NR off and existing NR modes where native symbols are available.
close_reason: Added WDSP-backed synthetic NR5 weak-signal fixture regression tests covering weak carrier, SSB-like speech, fading carrier, impulse noise with NB off/on, and noise-only stability. Fixed SPNR weak-signal preservation for stationary CW/voice-like content with narrow-tone protection and diagnostics-gated dry recovery. Rebuilt the Win x64 test runtime DLL and verified native exports, full DSP tests, full solution build, and full solution tests.

title: Expose TX stage density and headroom diagnostics
description: /api/tx/diag exposes raw WDSP TXA stage peaks/averages and gain reduction, but live tuning still requires the operator or remote diagnostics agent to infer whether the transmit waveform is under-filled, dense, clipping-risk, or being held down by ALC/CFC. Add derived, read-only TX stage density/headroom diagnostics so realtime monitoring can tune studio-quality TX audio without duplicating existing raw meters.
acceptance_criteria: TX stage diagnostics include output headroom, output crest factor, density status, density tone, and recommendation derived from existing TXA meters; idle/sentinel meters stay null/safe; focused backend tests cover underfilled, optimized, clipping-risk, and ALC-heavy cases; frontend client normalization and Hardware Diagnostics TX section expose the new fields.
close_reason: Exposed derived TXA density/headroom diagnostics in API, frontend, and focused tests.

title: Expose Smart NR runtime alignment diagnostics
description: Live Smart NR diagnostics show frontend scene recommendations and backend WDSP requested/effective NR modes, but no explicit alignment verdict. Add a non-duplicative alignment status to /api/dsp/nr-condition so real-time tuning can tell whether the suggested NR profile is actually active or still pending/mismatched.
acceptance_criteria: NR condition DTO includes expected runtime mode, alignment status, boolean alignment, and recommendation; backend tests cover aligned NR2, pending/mismatched NR4/Off, and notch/light cases; frontend normalizer preserves the new fields.
close_reason: Added Smart NR runtime alignment fields to /api/dsp/nr-condition, including expected WDSP mode, aligned boolean, status, and recommendation; exposed them in the Hardware Diagnostics DSP scene view; verified backend service/endpoint tests and frontend client normalization.

title: Design experimental NR5 weak-signal noise reduction
description:
Design and implement an experimental NR5 noise-reduction mode for weak-signal receive work. The goal is not to expose the existing RNNoise/NR3 path under a new name; NR3 is intentionally hidden because its generic speech model is not HF-signal aware. NR5 should preserve faint coherent signals while lowering stationary and slowly varying noise, with behavior suitable for SSB/CW weak-signal listening.

Initial design direction: build a new WDSP-native post-RXA stage that reuses EMNR's FFT/noise-estimation foundation and SBNR's psychoacoustic post-filter ideas, but adds a signal-preserving mask/salience gate so narrowband or slowly emerging signals are not over-subtracted. Keep it experimental until bench and on-air results show it actually improves copy over NR2/NR4.

Constraints: all NR modes are mutually exclusive in WDSP; the public NrMode enum is append-only because it is persisted; new DSP defaults are operator-visible and need maintainer sign-off; no new runtime dependency should be added without explicit approval.
design:
Proposed architecture:
1. Add a native WDSP stage (working name spnr/nr5) rather than composing NR2 and NR4 by enabling both, because RXA currently treats ANR/EMNR/RNNR/SBNR as mutually exclusive and RXAbp1Check/bp1 positioning depends on that model.
2. Reuse EMNR's overlap-add FFT frame shape, noise estimators, decision-directed a-priori SNR, and optional trained/zeta logic where practical.
3. Add signal-preservation logic before attenuation: track per-bin temporal coherence, local spectral peaks, and low-SNR persistence so faint carriers/CW tones/SSB formants can be held above the suppression floor.
4. Add a bounded post-filter inspired by SBNR/post2 to reduce musical noise without whitening away weak-signal identity.
5. Surface one conservative mode first (NR5 Experimental) with nullable advanced tunables later, following the NR2/NR4 lazy-default pattern.
6. Validate with synthetic IQ fixtures before UI polish: weak tone in AWGN, weak SSB-like modulated signal, fading carrier, impulsive noise with NB off/on, and silence/no-signal noise-floor stability.
acceptance_criteria:
- Design note documents algorithm, WDSP integration points, and expected tradeoffs versus NR2/NR4.
- Native stage builds on all supported platforms or degrades behind a compile-time flag/stub like NR3/NR4.
- C# P/Invoke layer can select NR5 without crashing when native symbols are missing.
- Existing NR1/NR2/NR4 mutual-exclusion tests are extended to include NR5.
- Synthetic weak-signal tests demonstrate NR5 preserves a buried tone or modulated signal better than the same chain with NR off, without broad clipping or instability.
- UI/persistence work is gated until the experimental backend is proven.

comments.author: Christian Suarez (N9WAR)
comments.text: Implemented first NR5/SPNR spike: native WDSP spnr stage with online per-bin noise learning, salience/presence protection, bounded post-NR normalizer; C# P/Invoke/availability guards; diagnostics fields wdspNr5SpnrAvailable/nr5Readiness; frontend NR5 selection and Smart NR weak-signal routing/fallback; design note at docs/nr5-spnr-design.md. Targeted verification passed (frontend Smart NR/API/controller tests, DSP NR tests, server NR-condition tests, direct MSVC compile of spnr.c). Full solution build is blocked by running OpenhpsdrZeus process locking app output DLLs; full frontend typecheck is blocked by unrelated pre-existing TS errors. Eight live samples stayed effective with fresh audio and rxMeters.status=agc-normalizing; RMS stayed about -18.0 to -18.6 dBFS and no SPNR/NR5 native errors appeared in logs. title: Refine idle/post-TX TX audio path diagnostics
description: Live TX diagnostics can show idle ring pressure after prior P2 activity. Separate post-TX/idle history from active keyed audio-path pressure so real-time tuning does not misclassify standby evidence as an on-air fault.
acceptance_criteria: Idle host with prior P2 activity and ring pressure reports a standby/post-TX status and recommendation; active keyed ring pressure remains a fault; focused TX diagnostics tests pass.
close_reason: Refined TX audio-path diagnostics so idle host with prior P2 activity and ring pressure reports post-tx-ring-pressure instead of generic idle pressure; active keyed ring pressure remains tx-ring-pressure. Verified focused TX diagnostics tests with isolated output.

title: Align Smart NR SSB weak-signal diagnostics with live scene evidence
description: Live /api/dsp/nr-condition readings on the connected G2 show coherent subthreshold SSB evidence while Smart NR publishes an NR2 profile with a clean/tonal reason. Refine Smart NR mode logic so weak sparse and coherent subthreshold SSB scenes produce explicit low-artifact weak-signal recommendations and diagnostics.

acceptance_criteria: SSB weak sparse and coherent subthreshold scenes report a weak-signal reason; Smart NR keeps low-artifact NR2 parameters for those scenes; targeted tests cover the live-diagnostics case.
close_reason: Live /api/dsp/nr-condition showed transient coherent subthreshold LSB evidence being reported with a clean/tonal Smart NR reason. Updated Smart NR SSB reasoning to emit explicit coherent weak-signal and weak-sparse NR2 explanations; added regression coverage and verified typecheck, full frontend tests, and production frontend build.

title: Refine Smart NR live diagnostics loop
description: Improve the Smart NR frontend DSP supervisor so live RX diagnostics and spectrum confidence produce stable, realtime recommendations without adding backend protocol or WDSP hot-path churn.
design: Keep changes inside zeus-web Smart NR/rx-chain diagnostics logic; no Zeus.Contracts wire-format changes, no WDSP/native changes, no audio scheduling changes.
acceptance_criteria: Smart NR avoids applying stale recommendations while diagnostics are stale or the scene is unstable; live diagnostics status explains held states; targeted frontend tests cover the new decision behavior.

title: Audit G2 ADC2 ground-on-TX and multi-receiver assignment surfaces
description: The ANAN G2 manual documents an option to ground ADC2 on TX, dual phase-synchronous ADCs with independent filter banks/6m LNAs, and 10 independent receivers assignable to either ADC. Audit Zeus settings/diagnostics against these G2 capabilities: expose or document ADC2 ground-on-TX if the protocol supports it, ensure ADC1/ADC2/preselector/RX2 routing diagnostics match the block diagram, and identify the gap between G2's 10-receiver capability and the current Zeus RX UI model.
acceptance_criteria: Zeus has a documented capability matrix for G2 ADC1/ADC2/RX2/preselector/6m-LNA routing; ADC2 ground-on-TX is either exposed with verified protocol wiring or explicitly marked unsupported; a concrete plan exists for more-than-RX2 receiver assignment if supported by the backend.
close_reason: Added G2 receiver topology diagnostics surface, Settings card, and endpoint coverage that marks ADC2 ground-on-TX and 10-DDC assignment as read-only gaps pending verified protocol mapping.

title: Add G2 external PureSignal feedback health diagnostics
description: The ANAN G2 manual says external PureSignal feedback for amplifier linearization should come from a coupler just above 0 dBm into RF Bypass, RF Bypass must be enabled in the antenna tab, and the G2 has no external amp ALC so drive/PA gain must prevent saturation. Build diagnostics that distinguish internal feedback from external RF Bypass feedback and warn on missing/low/high feedback, RF Bypass not enabled, or drive conditions that make PS correction untrustworthy.
acceptance_criteria: External PS mode shows RF Bypass/feedback path status, feedback level health, and actionable warnings based on the manual target; software drive/PA guidance avoids implying external amp ALC exists; PureSignal hot-path DSP logic is not changed without maintainer sign-off.
close_reason: Added read-only PureSignal feedback-path health diagnostics to /api/radio/diagnostics and Settings > Hardware Diagnostics. The snapshot now reports internal vs external/RF Bypass source, raw feedback level versus the calcc useful/centered windows, attenuation/HW peak state, calibration stall/correcting status, and G2-manual guidance that external feedback should enter RF Bypass from a coupler just above 0 dBm with no external amp ALC. Covered by backend endpoint tests and frontend normalization tests/typecheck/build.

title: Map G2 Dig In TX Disable and user digital inputs
description: The ANAN G2 manual documents the rear-panel Dig In stereo jack as CW key on tip and TX Disable on ring, while Zeus currently surfaces Protocol 2 userDigitalIn mostly as raw/labelled User I/O diagnostics. Confirm the firmware bit mapping for G2/Saturn userDigitalIn, then expose TX Disable as an explicit inhibit/diagnostic state and only wire TX-blocking behavior once the bit-level mapping is verified against hardware or Thetis/piHPSDR.
acceptance_criteria: G2 Dig In tip/ring bit mapping is documented; TX Disable appears in Hardware Diagnostics/Settings with clear inactive/active status; any TX inhibit behavior is gated to verified boards and covered by tests or bench notes; no duplicate OC-line work is introduced.
close_reason: Mapped G2 Dig In TX Disable from Thetis P2 HPSP 1025 byte 59 bit 1 / Zeus userDigital1, added read-only /api/radio/dig-in diagnostics and Settings card, and kept TX blocking unarmed pending explicit hardware-inhibit policy.

title: Verify live P2 TX egress diagnostics after restart
description: After restarting OpenhpsdrZeus so commit e694bfc7 is loaded, transmit into a safe load/antenna and confirm /api/tx/diag.protocol2 reports inputComplexSamples, packetsQueued, packetsSent, queuedPackets, failures, lastPacketsPerSecond, and FIFO model in real time. Use those counters with TX meters to tune audio density without relying on P1 ring counters.
notes: 2026-06-15: Sampled live desktop backend on 127.0.0.1:6061 after restart; P2 DUC counters were present but TX was idle/stale during the sample window. Added /api/tx/diag egress health summary so operators can distinguish p2-live vs p2-stale/waiting/failure without inferring from raw counters. Leave this issue open for an actual keyed TX validation pass.

title: P2 [zeus] Bypass TX audio plugin chain (AudioChain) for TCI / remote TX audio sources
description:
Investigate + implement bypass of the entire TX audio plugin insert chain (the AudioChain / IAudioPlugin slots driven by AudioPluginBridge) when the active TX audio source is a remote TCI client (or any external/remote driver) instead of the local mic.

## Why
When a TCI client (ExpertSDR3, MSHV, remote tunnel users, etc.) supplies TX audio via the TCI binary stream, that audio has already been processed (or deliberately left clean) on the client side. Re-running the local Audio Suite plugins (EQ, compressor, noise gate, VST effects, etc.) produces double-processing, wrong timbre for voice, and broken behaviour for digital modes.

Today the TX plugin seam is source-agnostic: every block that reaches WdspDspEngine.ProcessTxBlock goes through the installed TxAudioBlockHandler → AudioChain, regardless of whether it came from browser mic, NativeMicCapture, WAV playback, or TciTxAudioReceiver.

## Current state (investigation summary)
- All TX audio sources funnel through TxAudioIngest (OnMicPcmBytesFromTci / FromMic / FromWav) with 500 ms hysteresis to keep sources from mixing.
- TxAudioIngest calls engine.ProcessTxBlock → WdspDspEngine (Zeus.Dsp/Wdsp/WdspDspEngine.cs:2417) does the volatile read of _txAudioPluginHandler and invokes AudioPluginBridge.Process if present.
- The handler always runs the _chain (TX AudioChain) before fexchange2. The chain only respects the global MasterBypassed + per-slot bypass today.
- TCI source awareness exists: TciSession._txSourceIsTci (set on TRX:... ,tci; command, cleared on plain trx or MOX fall). Exposed as TxSourceIsTci. Used only for TX_CHRONO pacing and accepting audio frames in that session. Not visible to the audio ingest or plugin bridge.
- Existing bypass (AudioChain.MasterBypassed via AudioChainMasterBypassService) is efficient (volatile + straight copy, no allocs, realtime safe) but is the operator's "Audio Suite" master toggle — not policy-driven by source.

See detailed findings in the session transcript for the investigation (key files: TxAudioIngest.cs, AudioPluginBridge.cs (the _chain vs _rxChain split), WdspDspEngine.cs:ProcessTxBlock + SetTxAudioPluginHandler, AudioChain.cs in Zeus.Plugins.Host, TciSession.cs:943 (the source flag), TciTxAudioReceiver.cs).

This is the TX-side counterpart to the RX post-demod plugin seam (zeus-3ar / feat/rx-audio-plugin-seam).

## Red-light / hot-path note
- Touches the TX audio hot path and the plugin seam (WdspDspEngine + DspPipelineService adjacent paths + AudioPluginBridge).
- Per CLAUDE.md + CONTRIBUTING.md hot paths section + the zeus-3ar issue itself: this is red-light. Architecture + signal-routing change. Requires maintainer sign-off before merge. Do not land without explicit approval.
- Do not touch PureSignal paths or the recently-shipped audio dropout scheduling without separate approval.

## Design options (record decisions here)
1. Automatic override of master bypass when TCI source is active (simple, reuses everything).
2. Separate volatile flag on AudioChain (ExternalSourceBypass / BypassForRemote) composed with MasterBypassed. Cleanest policy vs. operator intent.
3. Swap the installed TxAudioBlockHandler to a pure passthrough when TCI source takes over.
4. Propagate source information (or "applyUserPlugins" bool) down to the handler / AudioBlockContext so the chain (or individual plugins) can decide.

Distinguish insert chain (the thing we want to bypass for remote) vs. ITxAudioTapPlugin taps (recorders etc. probably still want to observe raw + air audio).

Prefer option 2 or 3 for minimal pollution of the operator's master-bypass state.

The bypass must be bit-identical pass-through when engaged (same requirement as the existing master bypass ADR).

## Scope
- Make the bypass automatic / policy-driven when the authoritative TX source is TCI (or future "remote" sources).
- Keep the operator's Audio Suite master bypass as an independent override (or document the interaction clearly).
- Surface the state somewhere (capability? diagnostic? UI hint when a TCI session is driving TX?).
- Preserve behaviour for local mic and for WAV playback (WAV is "local" in intent).
- Taps vs. inserts: be explicit.
- Tests: unit tests around AudioChain + a source-aware path in TxAudioIngest / bridge (similar to existing AudioPluginBridgeProcessLivePreviewTests and AudioChainTests).
- On-air: verify on HL2 (user rig) that local mic still gets plugins, TCI-driven TX does not.
- Docs: update relevant lesson or TCI docs if the behaviour becomes user-visible.

## Non-goals (for this issue)
- Changing WDSP internal TXA stages.
- Adding new plugins or the UI for this bypass.
- Remote TCI control surface work (that's the tunnel epic).
- Altering the RX seam.

## References
- Current branch at investigation time: feat/rx-audio-plugin-seam (zeus-3ar)
- TCI TX audio path: Tci/TciTxAudioReceiver.cs, TxAudioIngest.cs:201 (OnMicPcmBytesFromTci)
- Plugin seam: AudioPluginBridge.cs:210 (SetTx...), 225 (the Process that calls chain), WdspDspEngine.cs:2417
- Bypass impl: AudioChain.cs:144 (if MasterBypassed copy), AudioChainMasterBypassService.cs
- TCI source flag: TciSession.cs:86, 192 (TxSourceIsTci), 943 (where it is set from the TRX command)
design: Options 1-4 recorded in description. Prefer separate ExternalSourceBypass volatile on AudioChain composed in Process, driven from TxAudioIngest's TCI recency / source flag. Or handler swap. Decision to be made in the issue after initial discussion. Do not mutate the operator-visible master bypass state without clear restore semantics.
acceptance_criteria: Automatic source-driven bypass of the TX insert plugin chain when TCI (or designated remote source) is supplying the audio; local mic and WAV playback continue to use the chain; operator master-bypass remains independent or clearly composed; bit-identical pass-through; hot-path safety preserved (no allocs/locks in audio thread); unit tests added; red-light maintainer sign-off obtained before merge; verified on HL2 that behaviour differs correctly by source.
notes:

RX is green-light (pure RX audio tap, no host change). TX depends on wiring IRadioController in the host (folded into PR #590) so a plugin can key MOX like the CW keyer does (MoxSource).
